discovery_objective: Determine whether AOF should prioritize a CLI context efficiency layer as the next release goal after v3.4.0.
key_questions: Which operator and AI tasks currently require reading too much CLI surface before a correct command can be chosen?
Is the core problem missing commands, or missing command taxonomy and routing metadata?
What is the smallest artifact and packet surface that improves discoverability without changing authority boundaries?
target_assumptions: AOF CLI is understandable but too expensive to reread in full for each session.
A structured command taxonomy plus registry would reduce context cost more effectively than adding new commands.
Recognition-packet integration can improve AI command selection without weakening backend-neutrality.
target_anomalies: CLI reference has grown broad enough that read/verify/write/execute/observe paths are not immediately visible.
Human readers and AI operators must infer command role from naming instead of a canonical registry.
Command surface maturity is higher than command-surface legibility.
target_user_or_market_slice: Self-hosting AOF operators and AI agents choosing runtime commands under context pressure.
stop_continue_pivot_signals: Continue if a small registry can improve command discovery without semantic breakage.
Pivot if the actual issue is command inconsistency rather than context routing.
Stop if the registry duplicates the CLI reference without improving selection paths.
notes: Proposal supplied after v3.4.0 release; evaluate as next-goal candidate rather than immediate implementation commitment.
